title: Here are four under the radar Red Sox players standing out in spring training
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Braiden Ward has 10 hits and 12 stolen bases in 12 games, leading all Red Sox in steals this spring.
Max Ferguson leads Red Sox in RBIs with seven, showing positional versatility at second base, shortstop, and center field.
Tayron Guerrero pitched six innings with six strikeouts and one earned run, potentially strengthening the Red Sox bullpen.

title: Once viewed as a rental, could Viktor Arvidsson be ideal ‘bridge’ forward for Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Viktor Arvidsson ranks second on the Bruins in 5-on-5 goals and third in 5-on-5 points this season.
Despite being a pending free agent, Bruins retained Arvidsson at trade deadline, valuing his scoring ability and veteran presence.
The Bruins consider a three-year extension for Arvidsson, who may serve as a short-term solution during the team's retooling phase.

title: 5 interior offensive linemen options for Patriots after Garrett Bradbury trade
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots trade center Garrett Bradbury to Bears, prompting left guard search this offseason.
Jared Wilson likely to move from left guard to center, creating need for new left guard.
Isaac Seumalo, David Edwards, Joel Bitonio, Elgton Jenkins, and Emmanuel Pregnon are key options for the Patriots.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for the ‘amazing’ Make-A-Wish children
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jason Perry, 48, from Stockton, CA, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Make-A-Wish Foundation.
Perry aims to support wish children in Greater Massachusetts-Rhode Island, inspired by their resilience amid health challenges.
Over 15 years, Perry has actively supported and met many wish children, some of whom continue to inspire him today.

title: Insider predicts Patriots sign Seahawks standout pass rusher, but don’t land Alec Pierce in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots could sign Seahawks edge rusher Boye Mafe for three years, $48 million with $25 million guaranteed, per insider prediction.
Mafe was pivotal in Seahawks' Super Bowl run, logging 44 pressures despite low sack numbers in 2025.
Insider predicts Colts' wide receiver Alec Pierce will join Chargers, not Patriots, on a three-year, $80 million deal.

title: Review & setlist: With sharp sarcasm, Jesse Welles took on war, bullies, and Trump at the House of Blues
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Jesse Welles performed at Boston's House of Blues on March 6, featuring his 'Under the Powerlines Tour'.
Welles, known for political satire, addressed topics like war, political figures, and social media with sharp lyrics.
The concert included 13 original songs and covers of Black Sabbath, Radiohead, John Denver, and more.

title: Masataka Yoshida hits a home run in Japan’s win vs. Korea, with Drake Maye in attendance
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit a 387-foot home run and secured three RBIs in Japan's 8-6 win over Korea.
Yoshida's performance included a two-run single in the seventh inning, helping Japan lead 2-0 in pool play.
Patriots quarterback Drake Maye attended the game in Japan, donning a Dodgers uniform while on a trip with his wife.

title: This Roslindale native is running her first Boston Marathon for Mass. Eye and Ear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Makayla Aboujaoude-Quirk, 25, will run her first Boston Marathon in 2026, supporting Mass. Eye and Ear.
Aboujaoude-Quirk is running with Team Eye and Ear, motivated by her own 10-year patient experience at the hospital.
Mass. Eye and Ear provides specialized care and has the only 24-hour emergency department for eye, ear, nose, and throat trauma in Massachusetts.

title: It was overdue, but Tim Legler was promoted to ESPN/ABC’s top NBA broadcast team this season, and he’s thriving
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Tim Legler, ESPN's longest-tenured NBA analyst, joined ESPN/ABC's top broadcast team for the 2025-26 season.
Legler, Mike Breen, and Richard Jefferson will call the NBA Finals, marking his first season in this prestigious role.
Legler praises Celtics' Joe Mazzulla and Jaylen Brown, noting interest in Jayson Tatum's return from injury.

title: Cooper Flagg was the Maine story in Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to action for the Celtics, scoring 15 points with 12 rebounds and 7 assists after an Achilles injury.
19-year-old Cooper Flagg debuted at TD Garden for the Dallas Mavericks, posting 16 points, 8 rebounds, and 6 assists.
Flagg, a Maine native, received significant cheering from Celtics fans despite playing for the opposition at TD Garden.
